catalysis_set.subject: biology
catalysis_set.question_text: If a living frog muscle is dissected out from a frog's body and placed in a closed container without oxygen, it can contract for a time when stimulated. Then it stops. But if the container is opened, it soon regains the ability to contract. Comment.
choices: The muscle cells contract due to the presence of carbon dioxide.
The muscle contracts because of the decrease in temperature when the container is opened.
The muscle cells are reanimated by the light entering the container when it is opened.
The muscle cells contract due to the release of lactic acid in the absence of oxygen.
The muscle cells require the presence of an inert gas, such as argon, to contract.
The muscle cells need nitrogen to contract.
The muscle cells contract due to the introduction of water.
The presence of oxygen enables the muscle cells to undergo aerobic respiration and produce more ATP, regaining its ability to contract.
Muscle contraction is restored due to increased humidity within the container.
The muscle cells contract because of the introduction of a new energy source when the container is opened.
catalysis_set.correct_answer_text: The presence of oxygen enables the muscle cells to undergo aerobic respiration and produce more ATP, regaining its ability to contract.
catalysis_set.wrong_answer_text: The muscle cells contract due to the presence of carbon dioxide.
catalysis_set.token_hint_note: compute at experiment runtime using AutoTokenizer

catalysis_set.subject: history
catalysis_set.question_text:
This question refers to the following information.
"Permit us, then, earnestly to recommend these articles to the immediate and dispassionate attention of the legislatures of the respective states. Let them be candidly reviewed under a sense of the difficulty of combining in one system the various sentiments and interests of a continent divided into so many sovereign and independent communities, under a conviction of the absolute necessity of uniting all our councils and all our strength, to maintain and defend our common liberties…."
Journals of the Continental Congress, 1777
A major weakness of the Articles of Confederation was that they
choices: made it too difficult for the government to raise money through taxes and duties
did not include a provision for creating new states
established a single, unified national identity too quickly
denied the federal government the power to mediate disputes between states
granted too much power to the judicial branch
failed to establish a national currency
did not include a mechanism for their own amendment
did not provide for a national army or navy
allowed for the unrestricted movement of goods and people between states
catalysis_set.correct_answer_text: made it too difficult for the government to raise money through taxes and duties
catalysis_set.wrong_answer_text: did not include a provision for creating new states
catalysis_set.token_hint_note: compute at experiment runtime using AutoTokenizer

catalysis_set.subject: chemistry
catalysis_set.question_text: Even though 1, 3, 5-trinitrobenzene (TNB) has more shattering power (more brisance) and is no more dangerous to handle, 2, 4, 6-trinitrotoluene (TNT) has always been the high ex-plosive in more general use. Can you suggest a reason (connected with manufacture) for the popularity of TNT? (Benzene and toluene are both readily available materials; for many years benzene was cheaper.)
choices: Toluene is more easily nitrated, and therefore more easily polynitrated than benzene.
The nitrating agents required for TNB are less readily available than those needed for TNT.
The production of TNB generates more harmful byproducts compared to TNT.
TNT is more stable than TNB.
Benzene is more easily nitrated than toluene.
The cost of manufacturing TNT is lower.
TNB is more sensitive to friction and shock, making it less safe to manufacture.
The purification process for TNB is more costly and time-consuming than that for TNT.
The chemical process for synthesizing TNB is more complex and requires higher temperatures than TNT.
TNB has a shorter shelf life than TNT, making it less suitable for long-term storage.
catalysis_set.correct_answer_text: Toluene is more easily nitrated, and therefore more easily polynitrated than benzene.
catalysis_set.wrong_answer_text: The nitrating agents required for TNB are less readily available than those needed for TNT.
catalysis_set.token_hint_note: compute at experiment runtime using AutoTokenizer
